splits.definition: Highest leverage activity is generating market demand; everything else (ops, service, fulfillment) flows from brand visibility.
Creators and influencers drive all attention and trust in attention-based markets; leverage 3-5 big creators as brand ambassadors.
Demand for creative output is accelerating; production volume becomes a sustainable competitive moat.

definition: Market demand generation drives business growth; achieved via creator leverage and content volume.
rationale: Centroid bundles distinct levers (demand, creator leverage, volume) that belong in separate load-bearing claims; aggressive split recovers three orthogonal nodes.

definition: Intelligence is measured by the speed at which behavior changes under constant conditions, not innate capacity.
rationale: Tight semantic cluster (0.94 min sim); all four members express the same load-bearing claim with high cohesion; canonical captures shared definition.

splits.definition: Community generates recurring revenue by supplying monthly consumable inputs (education materials, resources) that anchor customer engagement.
Monthly recurring consumption of products anchors members to the platform and prevents churn.

definition: Community monetization via recurring resource supply drives monthly engagement and revenue.
rationale: Two orthogonal claims conflated: (1) business model (recurring revenue from monthly inputs), (2) retention mechanism (consumption anchors churn). Split recovers both.

splits.canonical_name: Market Saturation Narrative is Usually a Performance Problem
splits.definition: Founders declaring market saturation often misdiagnose weak execution or poor positioning; comparable geographies succeed with same offer.

splits.canonical_name: Market Selection Criteria: Growth + Pain + Targeting
splits.definition: Picking the right market (growing >20% CAGR, clear pain, targeting ease) matters more than offer strength or persuasion skill.

splits.canonical_name: Geographic Expansion Sequence: Dominate Local, Then Expand
splits.definition: Conquer one city fully before expanding to country/world; overexpansion before local dominance causes collapse.

splits.canonical_name: Market Tailwinds Enable Growth by Default
splits.definition: Growing markets (20%+ CAGR) enable returns via default growth; shrinking markets require heroic execution. Outsized returns exist during market imbalances.
Saturation is often false; markets have far more capacity than founders assume. Test broader hooks rather than optimize narrow channels.

definition: Market selection and expansion strategy; saturation is usually misdiagnosis of execution gaps.
rationale: Variance 0.098; min_sim 0.89 indicates loose cluster. Nine members express five distinct load-bearing ideas: (1) saturation myth, (2) selection criteria, (3) expansion sequence, (4) tailwinds, (5) capacity misjudgment. Aggressive split recovers all.

canonical_name: Effort Compounds Relative to Starting Conditions
aliases: Speed of Learning Over Starting Point
definition: Extreme work ethic can overcome natural ability disadvantages; focus on controllables (effort, improvement) not luck.
rationale: Centroid 'Work Ethic Substitution' is too narrow; cluster articulates broader claim: natural ability sets baseline, but effort + circumstance determine outcome. Rename to canonical.

canonical_name: Leadership Sets Culture; Culture Determines Acceptable Effort
definition: A leader sets the culture and acceptable effort standard; a declining unit reflects leadership problem, not market or team problem.
rationale: Tight cohesion (0.896 min sim); all six members converge on same load-bearing claim: leadership quality drives culture → execution quality → business results.

canonical_name: True CAC Differs from Reported CAC
definition: Reported CAC (e.g., $130) obscures true CAC after conversion bottlenecks (e.g., trial-to-paying rate); funnel problems masquerade as targeting problems.
rationale: Only two members but high cohesion (0.941 min sim). Rename from 'CAC vs. True CAC' to 'True CAC Differs' to emphasize the mechanism (discrepancy due to hidden conversion loss).

splits.canonical_name: Cross-Platform Retargeting Increases Touchpoints and Reduces CAC
splits.definition: Following customers across Google, Facebook, and other platforms increases touchpoints and lowers customer acquisition cost.

splits.canonical_name: Revenue Mix: Recurring, Paid Search, and SEO
splits.definition: Revenue splits across recurring (past customers), paid search (Google Ads), and organic (SEO); ratio varies by maturity.

splits.canonical_name: Platform Choice and Backup Channels Matter
splits.definition: Primary channel failure requires backup channels (Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn); platform monetization varies by audience quality.

definition: Multi-channel marketing requires retargeting, revenue diversification, and backup channel readiness.
rationale: Variance 0.083; centroid 'Cross-Platform Retargeting' bundles distinct claims: (1) retargeting mechanism, (2) revenue mix analysis, (3) platform selection. Split recovers all.

canonical_name: All Life Paths Have Equal Suffering; Outcome Differs by Choice
definition: All career paths involve equal suffering; the variable is what you get for that suffering, not whether you suffer.
rationale: Extremely tight cohesion (0.984 min sim); both members are semantic near-duplicates; canonical captures shared assertion.

definition: Each limit you break raises your belief in what you can handle; growth happens at capacity edge where pain signals stretching.
rationale: Cohesive cluster (0.895 min sim); all five members express same metaphorical claim: growth ≈ breaking previous limits; pain is signal of capacity expansion.

canonical_name: Scaling Performance Marketing Compresses Margins Until Breakeven
definition: Scaling direct response (paid ads) compresses margins as CAC rises, churn accelerates, and supply costs grow; eventually reaches unprofitable breakeven.
rationale: High cohesion (0.914 min sim); all seven members converge on one mechanistic claim: growth via arbitrage (spend < margin) is unsustainable at scale. Canonical captures mechanism.

canonical_name: Sales Productivity: Parallel Dialing and Show-Up Rate Optimization

splits.canonical_name: Parallel Dialer Maximizes Talk Time Over Waiting Time
splits.definition: Parallel dialer calling 10 numbers at ~5% pickup enables team to maximize talk time rather than waiting time between dials.

splits.canonical_name: Show-Up Rate and Lead Nurture Optimization
splits.definition: Booked calls have ~50% show-up baseline; lead nurture playbook improves show-up rate to ~70%.
Five micro-levers (parallel dialing, nurture, capacity, volume) enable teams to serve more clients in same space without opening new facility or hiring proportionally.

definition: Sales team productivity driven by parallel dialing, show-up rate optimization, and capacity maximization levers.
rationale: Variance 0.085; min_sim 0.90 indicates loose fit. Five members address three orthogonal sales productivity mechanisms; split recovers all.

canonical_name: High-Value Customers Are Less Demanding Per Dollar
definition: High-value customers are less price-sensitive and less demanding per revenue dollar (e.g., $100K is 1% of net worth vs. $100 is 10% of savings).
rationale: Cohesive cluster (0.894 min sim); all four members converge on same economic claim: customer quality inversely correlates with price sensitivity.

canonical_name: Undefined Words Create Mythology and Prevent Clear Thinking
definition: Undefined abstract terms in conversation (e.g., 'quality', 'success') create mythology; both parties use words without shared meaning.
rationale: Tight cohesion (0.950 min sim); all three members express same load-bearing claim: clarity requires explicit definitions before problem-solving.

canonical_name: The Execution Gap: Knowledge Without Action
aliases: The Execution Gap: Why Entrepreneurs Get Stuck
definition: Most people know what to do but don't act; requires three-tier accountability to bridge gap between knowledge and execution.
rationale: Centroid is good; rename slightly to emphasize the load-bearing claim: knowledge ≠ execution. Four members all address reasons execution fails (delay gratification, pain, activation, accountability).
